title: CircumventionProtocol
description: A deployed censorship-circumvention protocol or a dominant-Internet cover protocol — distilled metadata + a markdown body file.

id.description: Stable slug. Lowercase letters, digits, dashes. Convention for circumvention: <wire-protocol-name>[-<variant>]. Convention for cover entries: cover-<protocol-slug>.

kind.description: circumvention = a circumvention tool / wire protocol; cover = a dominant Internet protocol that circumvention tools may mimic to gain collateral-freedom protection.

family.description: For circumvention entries: originating / canonical implementation family. For cover entries: typically 'cover'.

status.description: Current operational status. Cover entries are virtually always 'active'.

implementations.description: Other repos shipping the same wire protocol (circumvention) or major real implementations (cover).

references.description: IDs of papers in circumvention-corpus that analyze this protocol.

internal_refs.description: IDs of docs in circumvention-corpus-private that analyze this protocol.

rfc.description: RFC numbers that standardize this protocol (cover entries; sometimes useful on circumvention entries too).

collateral_cost.description: Qualitative score of how disruptive a wholesale block of this protocol would be for the censor's economy / society.

crypto_surface.description: What a passive observer can vs. cannot see when this protocol is on the wire.

common_implementations.description: Major real implementations (browsers, OS clients, server stacks). Informs JA3/JA4 fingerprint diversity available.

mimics.description: (circumvention entries) Cover-protocol IDs in this catalog that the protocol mimics or rides over.

used_as_cover_by.description: (cover entries) Circumvention-protocol IDs in this catalog that mimic / ride over this cover.
